title: Mexican Quesadillas

subs.name: thin roti or paratha — the result is a desi take on quesadillas that many families already make
paneer, grated or crumbled, melts differently but works well
subs.notes: mix with a little butter for creaminess

subs.name: corn kernels or mushrooms for a different filling
tomato ketchup thinned with lime juice for a simpler dip

steps.text: Heat oil in a frying pan over medium-high flame. Add the sliced onion and capsicum with a pinch of salt and cook, stirring often, for 5–6 minutes until softened and lightly caramelised at the edges. Stir in the garlic, green chilli and cumin for the last minute, then take off the heat.

Lay a tortilla flat on a dry tawa over medium flame. Scatter a quarter of the grated cheese over one half of the tortilla, then spoon a quarter of the capsicum mixture on top. Sprinkle with coriander leaves and fold the plain half over the filling, pressing gently.

Toast the quesadilla for 2 minutes until the underside is golden and crisp. Carefully flip with a spatula and toast the second side for another 2 minutes until the cheese is fully melted and both sides are evenly browned.

Slide onto a board and repeat with the remaining tortillas and filling. Cut each quesadilla into three wedges with a knife or pizza cutter. Serve immediately with thick curd or a drizzle of chilli sauce on the side.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: cheese → paneer, grated or crumbled, melts differently but works well (mix with a little butter for creaminess); tortilla → thin roti or paratha — the result is a desi take on quesadillas that many families already make; capsicum → corn kernels or mushrooms for a different filling; curd → tomato ketchup thinned with lime juice for a simpler dip
indian_kitchen_adaptation: A tawa is the perfect tool here — use a cast-iron tawa or heavy flat pan on medium flame. Amul or any processed cheese works if mozzarella is unavailable; grated paneer mixed with a pinch of salt and pepper is a good vegetarian substitute. Thin wheat rotis from any Indian kitchen replace flour tortillas seamlessly.
